text.title: RACI Matrix Examples
text.summary: Concrete RACI examples for a feature development workflow, a solopreneur-with-contractors engagement, and an agent-driven project.

example.code: | Activity | PM | Product | Engineering | Design | QA | |--------------------|----|---------|-------------|--------|----| | Define requirements| C | A | C | C | I | | Create mockups | I | C | I | R | I | | Approve design | I | A | C | R | I | | Write code | I | C | R | I | I | | Code review | I | I | A | I | I | | Write tests | I | I | C | I | R | | Deploy to staging | C | I | R | I | C | | UAT sign-off | I | A | I | I | R | | Production deploy | A | I | R | I | C |
example.note: One A per row throughout. Engineering is A on code review (peer accountability). PM is A on production deploy (risk ownership). Consulted count is low — maximum 2 per row.

example.code: | Task | Owner | Designer | Developer | VA | |----------------|-------|----------|-----------|-----| | Product vision | A/R | I | I | I | | UI design | A | R | C | I | | Development | A | C | R | I | | Testing | A | I | C | R | | Launch | A/R | I | I | C |
example.note: A/R combined is acceptable for the solopreneur context where the single owner is both the decision-maker and executor. The pattern avoids the "A also = R fudge" anti-pattern because the intent is explicit.

example.code: | Task | PM (human) | claude-agent | QA (human) | |-------------------------|------------|--------------|------------| | Generate issue hierarchy| A | R | I | | Triage incoming issues | A | R | C | | Transition issue states | C | R | I | | Approve bulk changes | A | C | I | | Retro on process policy | A | C | R |
example.note: The agent is always R (does the work) but never A (never the final decision-maker). Human PM holds A for all tasks involving policy or irreversible changes. Explicitly prohibit the agent from being A in the system prompt.

antipattern.description: Assigning an AI agent as Accountable for any task in a RACI matrix.
antipattern.reason: Agents lack authority, legal accountability, and organizational standing. A-role requires a human who can be held responsible for outcomes.
